docstring.text:
Provide functionality for storing and retrieving conversation history and embeddings.

This package defines the core `MemoryInterface` and concrete implementations for different storage backends.
Represents the attack result data in the database.

params.name: __tablename__
params.desc: The name of the database table.

params.name: __table_args__
params.desc: Additional arguments for the database table.

params.name: id
params.desc: The unique identifier for the attack result entry.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The unique identifier of the conversation that produced this result.

params.name: objective
params.desc: Natural-language description of the attacker's objective.

params.name: attack_identifier
params.desc: Identifier of the attack (e.g., name, module).

params.name: objective_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the objective.

params.name: last_response_id
params.desc: Foreign key to the last response MessagePiece.

params.name: last_score_id
params.desc: Foreign key to the last score ScoreEntry.

params.name: executed_turns
params.desc: Total number of turns that were executed.

params.name: execution_time_ms
params.desc: Total execution time of the attack in milliseconds.

params.name: outcome
params.desc: The outcome of the attack, indicating success, failure, or undetermined.

params.name: outcome_reason
params.desc: Optional reason for the outcome, providing additional context.

params.name: attack_metadata
params.desc: Metadata can be included as key-value pairs to provide extra context.

params.name: pruned_conversation_ids
params.desc: List of conversation IDs that were pruned from the attack.

params.name: adversarial_chat_conversation_ids
params.desc: List of conversation IDs used for adversarial chat.

params.name: timestamp
params.desc: The timestamp of the attack result entry.

params.name: last_response
params.desc: Relationship to the last response prompt memory entry.

params.name: last_score
params.desc: Relationship to the last score entry.

docstring.text:
Filter a dictionary to only include JSON-serializable values.

This function iterates through the metadata dictionary and keeps only
values that can be serialized to JSON, discarding any non-serializable objects.

params.name: metadata
params.desc: Dictionary with potentially non-serializable values

returns.desc: Dictionary with only JSON-serializable values

docstring.text: Convert this database entry back into an AttackResult object.

returns.desc: The reconstructed attack result including related conversations and scores.

docstring.text: Initialize an AttackResultEntry from an AttackResult object.

params.name: entry
params.desc: The attack result object to convert into a database entry.

docstring.text:
A class to manage conversation memory using Azure SQL Server as the backend database. It leverages SQLAlchemy Base
models for creating tables and provides CRUD operations to interact with the tables.

This class encapsulates the setup of the database connection, table creation based on SQLAlchemy models,
and session management to perform database operations.
Insert a list of message pieces into the memory storage.
Dispose the engine and clean up resources.
Fetch all entries from the specified table and returns them as model instances.

returns.desc: Sequence[EmbeddingDataEntry]: A sequence of EmbeddingDataEntry instances representing all stored embeddings.

docstring.text:
Azure SQL implementation: lightweight aggregate stats per conversation.

Executes a single SQL query that returns message count (distinct
sequences), a truncated last-message preview, the first non-empty
labels dict, and the earliest timestamp for each conversation_id.

params.name: conversation_ids
params.desc: The conversation IDs to query.

returns.desc: Mapping from conversation_id to ConversationStats.

docstring.text: Provide a session for database operations.

returns.desc: A new SQLAlchemy session bound to the configured engine.

docstring.text:
Azure SQL implementation: extract unique class_name values from
the atomic_attack_identifier JSON column.

returns.desc: Sorted list of unique attack class name strings.

docstring.text:
Azure SQL implementation: extract unique converter class_name values
from the request_converter_identifiers array in the atomic_attack_identifier
JSON column.

returns.desc: Sorted list of unique converter class name strings.

docstring.text: Drop and recreate existing tables.
Initialize an Azure SQL Memory backend.

params.name: connection_string
params.desc:
The connection string for the Azure Sql Database. If not provided,
it falls back to the 'AZURE_SQL_DB_CONNECTION_STRING' environment variable.

params.name: results_container_url
params.desc:
The URL to an Azure Storage Container. If not provided,
it falls back to the 'AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_DB_DATA_CONTAINER_URL' environment variable.

params.name: results_sas_token
params.desc:
The Shared Access Signature (SAS) token for the storage container.
If not provided, falls back to the 'AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_DB_DATA_SAS_TOKEN' environment variable.

params.name: verbose
params.desc: Whether to enable verbose logging for the database engine. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Provide a centralized memory instance across the framework.
The provided memory instance will be reused for future calls.
Return a centralized memory instance.

returns.desc: The singleton memory instance.

raises.desc: If the central memory instance has not been set.

docstring.text: Set a provided memory instance as the central instance for subsequent calls.

params.name: passed_memory
params.desc: The memory instance to set as the central instance.

docstring.text:
Represents the embedding data associated with conversation entries in the database.
Each embedding is linked to a specific conversation entry via an id.

params.name: id
params.desc: The primary key, which is a foreign key referencing the UUID in the PromptMemoryEntries table.

params.name: embedding
params.desc: An array of floats representing the embedding vector.

params.name: embedding_type_name
params.desc: The name or type of the embedding, indicating the model or method used.

docstring.text: The MemoryEmbedding class is responsible for encoding the memory embeddings.

params.name: embedding_model
params.desc: An instance of a class that supports embedding generation.

docstring.text: Generate metadata for a message piece.

params.name: message_piece
params.desc: the message piece for which to generate a text embedding

returns.desc: The generated metadata.

raises.desc: If the message piece is not of type text.

docstring.text: Initialize the memory embedding helper with a backing embedding model.

params.name: embedding_model
params.desc:
The embedding model used to
generate text embeddings. If not provided, a ValueError is raised.

docstring.text:
Handles the export of data to various formats, currently supporting only JSON format.
This class utilizes the strategy design pattern to select the appropriate export format.
Export the provided data to a file in the specified format.

params.name: data
params.desc: The data to be exported, as a list of MessagePiece instances.

params.name: file_path
params.desc: The full path, including the file name, where the data will be exported.

params.name: export_type
params.desc: The format for exporting data. Defaults to "json".

raises.desc: If no file_path is provided or if the specified export format is not supported.

docstring.text:
Export the provided data to a CSV file at the specified file path.
Each item in the data list, representing a row from the table,
is converted to a dictionary before being written to the file.

params.name: data
params.desc: The data to be exported, as a list of MessagePiece instances.

params.name: file_path
params.desc: The full path, including the file name, where the data will be exported.

docstring.text:
Export the provided data to a JSON file at the specified file path.
Each item in the data list, representing a row from the table,
is converted to a dictionary before being written to the file.

params.name: data
params.desc: The data to be exported, as a list of MessagePiece instances.

params.name: file_path
params.desc: The full path, including the file name, where the data will be exported.

docstring.text:
Export the provided data to a Markdown file at the specified file path.
Each item in the data list is converted to a dictionary and formatted as a table.

params.name: data
params.desc: The data to be exported, as a list of MessagePiece instances.

params.name: file_path
params.desc: The full path, including the file name, where the data will be exported.

raises.desc: If no file_path is provided or if there is no data to export.

docstring.text:
Initialize the MemoryExporter.

Sets up the available export formats using the strategy design pattern.

Abstract interface for conversation memory storage systems.

This interface defines the contract for storing and retrieving chat messages
and conversation history. Implementations can use different storage backends
such as files, databases, or cloud storage services.

Insert a list of attack results into the memory storage.
The database model automatically calculates objective_sha256 for consistency.

Add attack results to an existing scenario result in memory.

This method efficiently updates a scenario result by appending new attack results
to a specific atomic attack name without requiring a full retrieve-modify-save cycle.

params.name: scenario_result_id
params.desc: The ID of the scenario result to update.

params.name: atomic_attack_name
params.desc: The name of the atomic attack to add results for.

params.name: attack_results
params.desc: The attack results to add.

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False otherwise.

docstring.text: Insert a list of message pieces into the memory storage.

Insert a list of message pieces into the memory storage.

Automatically updates the sequence to be the next number in the conversation.
If necessary, generates embedding data for applicable entries

params.name: request
params.desc: The message piece to add to the memory.

docstring.text: Insert a list of scenario results into the memory storage.

params.name: scenario_results
params.desc: Sequence of ScenarioResult objects to store in the database.

docstring.text: Insert a list of scores into the memory storage.
Insert a list of seed datasets into the memory storage.

params.name: datasets
params.desc: A list of seed datasets to insert.

params.name: added_by
params.desc: The user who added the datasets.

docstring.text: Insert a list of seed groups into the memory storage.

params.name: prompt_groups
params.desc: A list of prompt groups to insert.

params.name: added_by
params.desc: The user who added the prompt groups.

raises.desc: If a seed group does not have at least one seed.
If seed group IDs are inconsistent within the same seed group.

docstring.text: Insert a list of seeds into the memory storage.

params.name: seeds
params.desc: A list of seeds to insert.

params.name: added_by
params.desc: The user who added the seeds.

raises.desc: If the 'added_by' attribute is not set for each prompt.

docstring.text: Ensure cleanup on process exit.

Disable embedding functionality for the memory interface.

Sets the memory_embedding attribute to None, disabling any embedding operations.
Dispose the engine and clean up resources.

Duplicate a conversation for reuse.

This can be useful when an attack strategy requires branching out from a particular point in the conversation.
One cannot continue both branches with the same conversation ID since that would corrupt
the memory. Instead, one needs to duplicate the conversation and continue with the new conversation ID.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID with existing conversations.

returns.desc: The uuid for the new conversation.

docstring.text:
Duplicate a conversation, excluding the last turn. In this case, last turn is defined as before the last
user request (e.g. if there is half a turn, it just removes that half).

This can be useful when an attack strategy requires back tracking the last prompt/response pair.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID with existing conversations.

returns.desc: The uuid for the new conversation.

docstring.text:
Duplicate messages with a new conversation ID.

Each duplicated piece gets a fresh ``id`` and ``timestamp`` while
preserving ``original_prompt_id`` for tracking lineage.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The messages to duplicate.

returns.desc: Tuple of (new_conversation_id, duplicated_message_pieces).

docstring.text: Enable embedding functionality for the memory interface.

params.name: embedding_model
params.desc:
Optional embedding model to use. If not provided,
attempts to create a default embedding model from environment variables.

raises.desc: If no embedding model is provided and required environment

docstring.text:
Export conversation data with the given inputs to a specified file.
    Defaults to all conversations if no filters are provided.

params.name: attack_id
params.desc: The ID of the attack. Defaults to None.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The ID of the conversation. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_ids
params.desc:
A list of prompt IDs.
Defaults to None.

params.name: labels
params.desc: A dictionary of labels. Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_after
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent after this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_before
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent before this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: original_values
params.desc: A list of original values. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_values
params.desc: A list of converted values. Defaults to None.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: The data type to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: not_data_type
params.desc: The data type to exclude. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_value_sha256
params.desc:
A list of SHA256 hashes of converted values.
Defaults to None.

params.name: file_path
params.desc:
The path to the file where the data will be exported.
Defaults to None.

params.name: export_type
params.desc: The format of the export. Defaults to "json".

returns.desc: The path to the exported file.

docstring.text: Load all EmbeddingData from the memory storage handler.
Retrieve a list of AttackResult objects based on the specified filters.

params.name: attack_result_ids
params.desc: A list of attack result IDs. Defaults to None.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective to filter by (substring match). Defaults to None.

params.name: objective_sha256
params.desc:
A list of objective SHA256 hashes to filter by.
Defaults to None.

params.name: outcome
params.desc:
The outcome to filter by (success, failure, undetermined).
Defaults to None.

params.name: attack_class
params.desc:
Filter by exact attack class_name in attack_identifier.
Defaults to None.

params.name: converter_classes
params.desc:
Filter by converter class names.
Returns only attacks that used ALL specified converters (AND logic, case-insensitive).
Defaults to None.

params.name: targeted_harm_categories
params.desc:
A list of targeted harm categories to filter results by.
These targeted harm categories are associated with the prompts themselves,
meaning they are harm(s) we're trying to elicit with the prompt,
not necessarily one(s) that were found in the response.
By providing a list, this means ALL categories in the list must be present.
Defaults to None.

params.name: labels
params.desc:
A dictionary of memory labels to filter results by.
These labels are associated with the prompts themselves, used for custom tagging and tracking.
Defaults to None.

returns.desc: Sequence[AttackResult]: A list of AttackResult objects that match the specified filters.

docstring.text: Retrieve a list of Message objects that have the specified conversation ID.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID to match.

returns.desc: MutableSequence[Message]: A list of chat memory entries with the specified conversation ID.

docstring.text:
Return lightweight aggregate statistics for one or more conversations.

Computes per-conversation message count (distinct sequence numbers),
a truncated last-message preview, the first non-empty labels dict,
and the earliest message timestamp using efficient SQL aggregation
instead of loading full pieces.

params.name: conversation_ids
params.desc: The conversation IDs to query.

returns.desc: Mapping from conversation_id to ConversationStats.
Conversations with no pieces are omitted from the result.

docstring.text: Retrieve a list of MessagePiece objects based on the specified filters.

params.name: attack_id
params.desc: The ID of the attack. Defaults to None.

params.name: role
params.desc: The role of the prompt. Defaults to None.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The ID of the conversation. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_ids
params.desc:
A list of prompt IDs.
Defaults to None.

params.name: labels
params.desc: A dictionary of labels. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc:
The metadata associated with the prompt.
Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_after
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent after this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_before
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent before this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: original_values
params.desc: A list of original values. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_values
params.desc: A list of converted values. Defaults to None.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: The data type to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: not_data_type
params.desc: The data type to exclude. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_value_sha256
params.desc:
A list of SHA256 hashes of converted values.
Defaults to None.

returns.desc: Sequence[MessagePiece]: A list of MessagePiece objects that match the specified filters.

raises.desc:
If there is an error retrieving the prompts,
an exception is logged and an empty list is returned.

docstring.text: Retrieve scores attached to message pieces based on the specified filters.

params.name: attack_id
params.desc: The ID of the attack. Defaults to None.

params.name: role
params.desc: The role of the prompt. Defaults to None.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The ID of the conversation. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_ids
params.desc:
A list of prompt IDs.
Defaults to None.

params.name: labels
params.desc: A dictionary of labels. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc:
The metadata associated with the prompt.
Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_after
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent after this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_before
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent before this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: original_values
params.desc: A list of original values. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_values
params.desc: A list of converted values. Defaults to None.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: The data type to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: not_data_type
params.desc: The data type to exclude. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_value_sha256
params.desc:
A list of SHA256 hashes of converted values.
Defaults to None.

returns.desc: Sequence[Score]: A list of scores extracted from the message pieces.

docstring.text: Retrieve the request that produced the given response.

params.name: response
params.desc: The response message object to match.

returns.desc: The corresponding message object.

raises.desc: If the response is not from an assistant role or has no preceding request.

docstring.text: Retrieve a list of ScenarioResult objects based on the specified filters.

params.name: scenario_result_ids
params.desc:
A list of scenario result IDs.
Defaults to None.

params.name: scenario_name
params.desc:
The scenario name to filter by (substring match).
Defaults to None.

params.name: scenario_version
params.desc: The scenario version to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: pyrit_version
params.desc: The PyRIT version to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: added_after
params.desc:
Filter for scenarios completed after this datetime.
Defaults to None.

params.name: added_before
params.desc:
Filter for scenarios completed before this datetime.
Defaults to None.

params.name: labels
params.desc:
A dictionary of memory labels to filter by.
Defaults to None.

params.name: objective_target_endpoint
params.desc:
Filter for scenarios where the
objective_target_identifier has an endpoint attribute containing this value (case-insensitive).
Defaults to None.

params.name: objective_target_model_name
params.desc:
Filter for scenarios where the
objective_target_identifier has a model_name attribute containing this value (case-insensitive).
Defaults to None.

returns.desc: Sequence[ScenarioResult]: A list of ScenarioResult objects that match the specified filters.

docstring.text: Retrieve a list of Score objects based on the specified filters.

params.name: score_ids
params.desc: A list of score IDs to filter by.

params.name: score_type
params.desc: The type of the score to filter by.

params.name: score_category
params.desc: The category of the score to filter by.

params.name: sent_after
params.desc: Filter for scores sent after this datetime.

params.name: sent_before
params.desc: Filter for scores sent before this datetime.

returns.desc: Sequence[Score]: A list of Score objects that match the specified filters.

docstring.text: Return a list of all seed dataset names in the memory storage.

returns.desc: Sequence[str]: A list of unique dataset names.

docstring.text: Retrieve groups of seed prompts based on the provided filtering criteria.

params.name: value
params.desc: The value to match by substring.

params.name: value_sha256
params.desc: SHA256 hash of value to filter seed groups by.

params.name: dataset_name
params.desc: Name of the dataset to match exactly.

params.name: dataset_name_pattern
params.desc:
A pattern to match dataset names using SQL LIKE syntax.
Supports wildcards: % (any characters) and _ (single character).
Examples: "harm%" matches names starting with "harm", "%test%" matches names containing "test".
If both dataset_name and dataset_name_pattern are provided, dataset_name takes precedence.

params.name: data_types
params.desc: List of data types to filter seed prompts by

params.name: harm_categories
params.desc: List of harm categories to filter seed prompts by.

params.name: added_by
params.desc: The user who added the seed groups to filter by.

params.name: authors
params.desc: List of authors to filter seed groups by.

params.name: groups
params.desc: List of groups to filter seed groups by.

params.name: source
params.desc: The source from which the seed prompts originated.

params.name: seed_type
params.desc:
The type of seed to filter by ("prompt", "objective", or
"simulated_conversation").

params.name: is_objective
params.desc: Deprecated in 0.13.0. Use seed_type="objective" instead.

params.name: parameters
params.desc: List of parameters to filter by.

params.name: metadata
params.desc:
A free-form dictionary for tagging
prompts with custom metadata.

params.name: prompt_group_ids
params.desc: List of prompt group IDs to filter by.

params.name: group_length
params.desc: The number of seeds in the group to filter by.

returns.desc: Sequence[SeedGroup]: A list of `SeedGroup` objects that match the filtering criteria.

docstring.text: Retrieve a list of seed prompts based on the specified filters.

params.name: value
params.desc: The value to match by substring. If None, all values are returned.

params.name: value_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the value to match. If None, all values are returned.

params.name: dataset_name
params.desc: The dataset name to match exactly. If None, all dataset names are considered.

params.name: dataset_name_pattern
params.desc:
A pattern to match dataset names using SQL LIKE syntax.
Supports wildcards: % (any characters) and _ (single character).
Examples: "harm%" matches names starting with "harm", "%test%" matches names containing "test".
If both dataset_name and dataset_name_pattern are provided, dataset_name takes precedence.

params.name: data_types
params.desc:
List of data types to filter seed prompts by
(e.g., text, image_path).

params.name: harm_categories
params.desc: A list of harm categories to filter by. If None,

params.name: added_by
params.desc: The user who added the prompts.

params.name: authors
params.desc:
A list of authors to filter by.
Note that this filters by substring, so a query for "Adam Jones" may not return results if the record
is "A. Jones", "Jones, Adam", etc. If None, all authors are considered.

params.name: groups
params.desc: A list of groups to filter by. If None, all groups are considered.

params.name: source
params.desc: The source to filter by. If None, all sources are considered.

params.name: seed_type
params.desc:
The type of seed to filter by ("prompt", "objective", or
"simulated_conversation").

params.name: is_objective
params.desc: Deprecated in 0.13.0. Use seed_type="objective" instead.

params.name: parameters
params.desc:
A list of parameters to filter by. Specifying parameters effectively returns
prompt templates instead of prompts.

params.name: metadata
params.desc: A free-form dictionary for tagging prompts with custom metadata.

params.name: prompt_group_ids
params.desc: A list of prompt group IDs to filter by.

returns.desc: Sequence[SeedPrompt]: A list of prompts matching the criteria.

raises.desc: If both 'seed_type' and deprecated 'is_objective' parameters are specified.

docstring.text: Provide a SQLAlchemy session for transactional operations.

returns.desc: A SQLAlchemy session bound to the engine.

docstring.text:
Return sorted unique attack class names from all stored attack results.

Extracts class_name from the attack_identifier JSON column via a
database-level DISTINCT query.

returns.desc: Sorted list of unique attack class name strings.

docstring.text:
Return all unique label key-value pairs across attack results.

Labels live on ``PromptMemoryEntry.labels`` (the established SDK
path).  This method JOINs with ``AttackResultEntry`` to scope the
query to conversations that belong to an attack, applies DISTINCT
to reduce duplicate label dicts, then aggregates unique key-value
pairs in Python.

returns.desc: dict[str, list[str]]: Mapping of label keys to sorted lists of
unique values.

docstring.text:
Return sorted unique converter class names used across all attack results.

Extracts class_name values from the request_converter_identifiers array
within the attack_identifier JSON column via a database-level query.

returns.desc: Sorted list of unique converter class name strings.

docstring.text: Print the schema of all tables in the database.

Update specific fields of an existing AttackResultEntry identified by conversation_id.

This method queries for the raw database entry by conversation_id and updates
the specified fields in place, avoiding the creation of duplicate rows.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID of the attack result to update.

params.name: update_fields
params.desc:
A dictionary of column names to new values.
Valid fields include 'adversarial_chat_conversation_ids',
'pruned_conversation_ids', 'outcome', 'attack_metadata', etc.

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False if the entry was not found.

docstring.text: Update specific fields of an existing AttackResultEntry identified by its primary key.

params.name: attack_result_id
params.desc: The UUID primary key of the AttackResultEntry.

params.name: update_fields
params.desc: Column names to new values.

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False if the entry was not found.

docstring.text: Update the labels of prompt entries in memory for a given conversation ID.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID of the entries to be updated.

params.name: labels
params.desc: New dictionary of labels.

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False otherwise.

docstring.text: Update prompt entries for a given conversation ID with the specified field values.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID of the entries to be updated.

params.name: update_fields
params.desc: A dictionary of field names and their new values (ex. {"labels": {"test": "value"}})

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False otherwise.

raises.desc: If update_fields is empty or not provided.

docstring.text: Update the metadata of prompt entries in memory for a given conversation ID.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The conversation ID of the entries to be updated.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc: New metadata.

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False otherwise.

docstring.text: Update the run state of an existing scenario result.

params.name: scenario_result_id
params.desc: The ID of the scenario result to update.

params.name: scenario_run_state
params.desc:
The new state for the scenario
(e.g., "CREATED", "IN_PROGRESS", "COMPLETED", "FAILED").

returns.desc: True if the update was successful, False otherwise.

docstring.text: Initialize the MemoryInterface.

params.name: embedding_model
params.desc:
If set, this includes embeddings in the memory entries
which are extremely useful for comparing chat messages and similarities,
but also includes overhead.

docstring.text:
Represents the prompt data.

Because of the nature of database and sql alchemy, type ignores are abundant :)

params.name: __tablename__
params.desc: The name of the database table.

params.name: __table_args__
params.desc: Additional arguments for the database table.

params.name: id
params.desc: The unique identifier for the memory entry.

params.name: role
params.desc: system, assistant, user

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The identifier for the conversation which is associated with a single target.

params.name: sequence
params.desc:
The order of the conversation within a conversation_id.
Can be the same number for multi-part requests or multi-part responses.

params.name: timestamp
params.desc: The timestamp of the memory entry.

params.name: labels
params.desc: The labels associated with the memory entry. Several can be standardized.

params.name: targeted_harm_categories
params.desc: The targeted harm categories for the memory entry.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc:
The metadata associated with the prompt. This can be specific to any scenarios.
Because memory is how components talk with each other, this can be component specific.
e.g. the URI from a file uploaded to a blob store, or a document type you want to upload.

params.name: converters
params.desc: The converters for the prompt.

params.name: prompt_target
params.desc: The target for the prompt.

params.name: attack_identifier
params.desc: The attack identifier for the prompt.

params.name: original_value_data_type
params.desc: The data type of the original prompt (text, image)

params.name: original_value
params.desc: The text of the original prompt. If prompt is an image, it's a link.

params.name: original_value_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the original prompt data.

params.name: converted_value_data_type
params.desc: The data type of the converted prompt (text, image)

params.name: converted_value
params.desc: The text of the converted prompt. If prompt is an image, it's a link.

params.name: converted_value_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the original prompt data.

params.name: idx_conversation_id
params.desc: The index for the conversation ID.

params.name: original_prompt_id
params.desc: The original prompt id. It is equal to id unless it is a duplicate.

params.name: scores
params.desc: The list of scores associated with the prompt.

docstring.text: Convert this database entry back into a MessagePiece object.

returns.desc: The reconstructed message piece with all its data and scores.

docstring.text: Initialize a PromptMemoryEntry from a MessagePiece.

params.name: entry
params.desc: The message piece to convert into a database entry.

docstring.text:
A memory interface that uses SQLite as the backend database.

This class provides functionality to insert, query, and manage conversation data
using SQLite. It supports both file-based and in-memory databases.

Note: this is replacing the old DuckDB implementation.
Insert a list of message pieces into the memory storage.
Dispose the engine and close all connections.

Export all table data using the specified exporter.

Iterate over all tables, retrieves their data, and exports each to a file named after the table.

params.name: export_type
params.desc: The format to export the data in (defaults to "json").

docstring.text: Export conversations and their associated scores from the database to a specified file.

returns.desc: The path to the exported file.

docstring.text: Fetch all entries from the specified table and returns them as model instances.

returns.desc: Sequence[EmbeddingDataEntry]: A sequence of EmbeddingDataEntry instances representing all stored embeddings.

docstring.text: Return a list of all table models used in the database by inspecting the Base registry.

returns.desc: list[Base]: A list of SQLAlchemy model classes.

docstring.text:
SQLite implementation: lightweight aggregate stats per conversation.

Executes a single SQL query that returns message count (distinct
sequences), a truncated last-message preview, the first non-empty
labels dict, and the earliest timestamp for each conversation_id.

params.name: conversation_ids
params.desc: The conversation IDs to query.

returns.desc: Mapping from conversation_id to ConversationStats.

docstring.text: Provide a SQLAlchemy session for transactional operations.

returns.desc: A SQLAlchemy session bound to the engine.

docstring.text:
SQLite implementation: extract unique class_name values from
the atomic_attack_identifier JSON column.

returns.desc: Sorted list of unique attack class name strings.

docstring.text:
SQLite implementation: extract unique converter class_name values
from the request_converter_identifiers array in the atomic_attack_identifier
JSON column.

returns.desc: Sorted list of unique converter class name strings.

docstring.text: Print the schema of all tables in the SQLite database.
Drop and recreates all tables in the database.
Initialize the SQLiteMemory instance.

params.name: db_path
params.desc:
Path to the SQLite database file.
Defaults to "pyrit.db".

params.name: verbose
params.desc:
Whether to enable verbose logging.
Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Represents the raw prompt or prompt template data as found in open datasets.

Note: This is different from the PromptMemoryEntry which is the processed prompt data.
SeedPrompt merely reflects basic prompts before plugging into attacks,
running through models with corresponding attack strategies, and applying converters.
PromptMemoryEntry captures the processed prompt data before and after the above steps.

params.name: __tablename__
params.desc: The name of the database table.

params.name: __table_args__
params.desc: Additional arguments for the database table.

params.name: id
params.desc: The unique identifier for the memory entry.

params.name: value
params.desc: The value of the seed prompt.

params.name: value_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the value of the seed prompt data.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: The data type of the seed prompt.

params.name: dataset_name
params.desc: The name of the dataset the seed prompt belongs to.

params.name: harm_categories
params.desc: The harm categories associated with the seed prompt.

params.name: description
params.desc: The description of the seed prompt.

params.name: authors
params.desc: The authors of the seed prompt.

params.name: groups
params.desc: The groups involved in authoring the seed prompt (if any).

params.name: source
params.desc: The source of the seed prompt.

params.name: date_added
params.desc: The date the seed prompt was added.

params.name: added_by
params.desc: The user who added the seed prompt.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc:
The metadata associated with the seed prompt. This includes
information that is useful for the specific target you're probing, such as encoding data.

params.name: parameters
params.desc:
The parameters included in the value.
Note that seed prompts do not have parameters, only prompt templates do.
However, they are stored in the same table.

params.name: prompt_group_id
params.desc:
The ID of a group the seed prompt may optionally belong to.
Groups are used to organize prompts for multi-turn conversations or multi-modal prompts.

params.name: sequence
params.desc:
The turn of the seed prompt in a group. When entire multi-turn conversations
are stored, this is used to order the prompts.

params.name: role
params.desc: The role of the prompt (e.g., user, system, assistant).

params.name: seed_type
params.desc: The type of seed - "prompt", "objective", or "simulated_conversation".

params.name: is_objective
params.desc: Deprecated in 0.13.0. Use seed_type="objective" instead.

docstring.text: Convert this database entry back into a Seed object.

returns.desc: The reconstructed seed object (SeedPrompt, SeedObjective, or SeedSimulatedConversation)

docstring.text: Initialize a SeedEntry from a Seed object.

params.name: entry
params.desc: The seed object to convert into a database entry.
